content.full_text:
April 27, 1989


Barbara Milman
Chief Consultant
Assembly Elections Committee
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                        Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                             Our File No. A-89-219

Dear Ms. Milman:

     We have received your letter of April 10, 1989 seeking
written confirmation of the advice I provided in our recent
telephone conversation.  This is to confirm that your letter
accurately reflects that advice.  A copy of your letter is
enclosed for your convenience.

     If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        John G. McLean
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:JGM:ld




428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

STATE CAPITOL
P.O. BOX 942849
SACRAMENTO CA 94249-0001
(916) 445-7905

DISTRICT OFFICE
1129 G STREET
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101
(619) 232-2405

IRMA MUNOZ
CHIEF OF STAFF

J. ERIC HEREFORD
FIELD REPRESENTATIVE

KATHLEEN McINTOSH
FIELD REPRESENTATIVE

Assembly
California Legislature

COMMITTEES
FINANCE AND INSURANCE
GOVERNMENTAL EFFICIENCY
CONSUMER PROTECTION
HOUSING AND COMMUNITY
DEVELOPMENT
RULES
RELATIONS AND INTER-
NATIONAL TRADE
JOINT COMMITTEE ON THE
STATES ECONOMY

PETER R. CHACON
ASSEMBLYMAN SEVENTY-NINTH DISTRICT

SAN DIEGO, ENCANTO, LEMON GROVE, PARADISE HILLS
& PORTIONS OF BONITA & SPRING VALLEY

CHAIRMAN, ELECTIONS, REAPPORTIONMENT AND
CONSTITUTIONAL AMENDMENTS

April 10, 1989

Mr. John McLean
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear John:

I am writing to confirm the oral advice you gave me
concerning the Cinco de Mayo celebration. You said that if
the Hispanic Legislative Caucus (which is comprised of the
seven Hispanic legislators) were to solicit contributions for
a reception for a Cinco de Mayo celebration, contributions to
the Caucus for the reception would be considered to be
contributions to the members of the Caucus for purposes of
Proposition 73.

Because of this, the Caucus has decided not to host a
celebration and reception for Scenic de Mayo. Instead, CAPE
(a nonprofit organization which is not affiliated with the
Caucus, and which is not a political committee) has decided
to put on a celebration and reception for Cinco de Mayo. The
Hispanic Legislative Caucus will be honored guests at the
celebration. Members of the community, including lobbyists
and other legislators and staff, will also be invited. CAPE
will raise the money for the reception, will arrange for the
reception, and will send out the invitations to the
celebration. Assemblyman Chacon has requested permission for
CAPE to use Capitol Park for the celebration. Members of the
legislature often make such requests for constituents.

It is my understanding of your advice that, in this
situation, contributions to CAPE for the reception will not
be considered contributions to the Caucus or to individual

Mr. John McLean
April 10, 1989
Page 2


members of the Caucus. Of course, the value to the
individuals attending the reception (food & drink) will be
gifts.

I would appreciate written confirmation of this advice.

Sincerely,

[signature]
BARBARA MILMAN
Chief Consultant

BM:lj

April 14, 1989


Barbara Milman
Office of Assemblymember Peter Chacon
State Capitol, Room 5119
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                          Re: Letter No. 89-219

Dear Ms. Milman:

     We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on April 11, 1989.  Your letter has
been assigned to John McLean for response. If you have any
questions, you may contact Mr. McLean directly at (916) 322-5901.

     If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response.  A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process.  If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you.  In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

     You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                          Sincerely,

                                          [signature]

                                          Diane M. Griffiths
                                          General Counsel

DMG:plh:confadv1




428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660
